user_request: Prepare a detailed plaint for recovery of Rs.15,00,000/- advanced as a hand loan by bank transfer. There is no written agreement. The transaction is supported by bank statement and WhatsApp communications. The Defendant has failed to repay despite repeated oral demands and legal notice. Plead limitation properly. Claim interest at 12% per annum from date of loan till realization. Draft strictly in accordance with Order VII CPC. Include proper cause of action, jurisdiction, valuation, court fee and prayer clause. Use formal Indian court language suitable for filing before City Civil Court, Bengaluru.

facts.summary: Recovery suit for Rs.15,00,000/- advanced as hand loan via bank transfer. Defendant defaulted despite oral demands and legal notice. Claim includes interest at 12% per annum.

chronology.event: Loan of Rs.15,00,000 advanced to Defendant via bank transfer as hand loan without written agreement
Repeated oral demands made to Defendant for repayment of loan
Legal notice served to Defendant demanding repayment
Defendant failed to repay despite legal notice and demands

evidence.description: Bank records evidencing transfer of Rs.15,00,000 to Defendant as hand loan
evidence.relevance: Primary evidence of loan transaction and amount

evidence.description: WhatsApp conversations between parties regarding loan and repayment demands
evidence.relevance: Corroborative evidence of loan acknowledgment and default

evidence.description: Legal notice served upon Defendant demanding repayment of loan amount with interest
evidence.relevance: Evidence of formal demand and cause of action

slots.value: Recovery of money based on hand loan

missing_fields: Exact date of loan transaction/bank transfer
Bank account details (account number, bank name, branch)
Whether any partial repayment was made
assumptions: Bank transfer records clearly identify Defendant as beneficiary
WhatsApp communications are preserved with metadata for authentication
Legal notice was served within limitation period

as to what a petition should contain. On the other hand the Code does not say that the ingredients required for 
plaint are the ingredients for a petition. Therefore, a petition is completely different from a plaint. Thus, a petition for

enforceable and court-fees for divorce petition is not Rs. 20/- but Re. 1/- as prescribed for petition.33

All necessary and material facts should be pleaded by the party, in support of the case set up by it. In the absence 
of pleadings, the evidence, if any, produced by the parties cannot be considered. No party should be permitted to 
travel beyond its pleading. The object and purpose of pleading is to enable the adversary party to know the case 
which it has to meet. In order to have a fair trial, it is imperative that the party should state the essential material 
facts, so that the other party may not be taken by surprise. However, the pleadings should receive a liberal 
construction, and no pedantic approach should be adopted to defeat judicial technicalities or hair-splitting. 
Sometimes, pleadings are expressed in words which may not expressly make out a case in accordance with strict 
interpretation of law. In such a case, it is the duty of the court to ascertain the substance of the pleadings (and not 
merely the form) in order to determine the case and the issues upon which the parties went to trial. If in spite of

IN THE CITY CIVIL COURT AT BENGALURU
CIVIL SUIT NO. {{SUIT_NUMBER}} OF {{SUIT_YEAR}}

{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}},
{{PLAINTIFF_AGE}} years, {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}}
... Plaintiff

Versus

{{DEFENDANT_NAME}},
{{DEFENDANT_AGE}} years, {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}}
... Defendant

PLAINT UNDER ORDER VII OF THE CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE, 1908

The Plaintiff, most respectfully submits as under:

1. DESCRIPTION OF PARTIES:
   (a) The Plaintiff is {{PLAINTIFF_DESCRIPTION}}.
   (b) The Defendant is {{DEFENDANT_DESCRIPTION}}.

2. That the Plaintiff and the Defendant are known to each other {{RELATIONSHIP_BETWEEN_PARTIES}}.

3. That on or about {{DATE_OF_LOAN}}, at {{PLACE_OF_TRANSACTION}}, the Defendant requested the Plaintiff to advance a hand loan of Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only) for {{PURPOSE_OF_LOAN}}. It is expressly stated that there is no written agreement executed between the parties in respect of the said loan, and the entire transaction was oral and based on mutual trust and confidence.

4. That pursuant to the said oral agreement and request, the Plaintiff, on {{DATE_OF_LOAN}}, transferred the sum of Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only) to the Defendant's bank account {{DEFENDANT_ACCOUNT_NUMBER}} maintained with {{BANK_NAME_AND_BRANCH}}, through electronic fund transfer (RTGS/NEFT/IMPS) bearing Transaction/Reference ID {{TRANSACTION_ID}}, as evidenced by the bank statement of the Plaintiff showing the debit entry.

5. That the said loan was advanced without any written instrument, promissory note, or security, and the Defendant orally undertook to repay the same on demand with interest at the rate of 12% per annum from the date of the loan until realization.

6. That subsequently, the Plaintiff required the said amount for his/her personal/business exigencies and made repeated oral demands upon the Defendant for repayment of the principal amount along with accrued interest, commencing from {{DATE_OF_FIRST_DEMAND}} and continuing thereafter at reasonable intervals, details and particulars of which are corroborated by digital communications including WhatsApp messages exchanged between the parties (copies whereof are annexed hereto as Annexure-B).

7. That despite the aforesaid repeated oral demands, the Defendant failed and neglected to repay the loan amount. Consequently, the Plaintiff, through his/her Advocate, served a legal notice dated {{DATE_OF_LEGAL_NOTICE}} upon the Defendant demanding repayment of the principal amount of Rs. 15,00,000/- along with interest at 12% per annum within the time stipulated therein. A true copy of the said legal notice along with the postal acknowledgment/proof of delivery is annexed hereto as Annexure-C.

8. That the Defendant received the said legal notice on {{DATE_OF_RECEIPT_OF_NOTICE}} but failed, neglected and/or refused to make any payment towards the principal or interest despite the expiry of the stipulated period, and has thus failed to discharge his/her legal obligation to repay the hand loan.

9. CAUSE OF ACTION:
   The cause of action first arose on {{DATE_OF_LOAN}} when the Defendant received the loan amount. The cause of action further arose on {{DATE_OF_FIRST_DEMAND}} and subsequent dates when oral demands were made and refused, and finally accrued on {{DATE_OF_RECEIPT_OF_NOTICE}} when the Defendant failed to comply with the legal notice demanding repayment. The present suit is filed well within the period of limitation prescribed under Article 21 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, being three years from the date when the loan was made.

10. JURISDICTION:
    This Hon'ble Court has territorial jurisdiction as the cause of action wholly or in part arose within the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court at {{PLACE_OF_CAUSE_OF_ACTION}}, Bengaluru. This Hon'ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to entertain and try the present suit as the value of the subject-matter of Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only) together with interest is within the pecuniary limits of the City Civil Court, Bengaluru.

11. VALUATION:
    The value of the subject-matter of the suit for the purpose of jurisdiction is Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only) together with interest thereon as claimed hereinbelow. For the purpose of court-fees, the suit is valued at Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only) and ad valorem court-fee has been paid thereon in accordance with the Karnataka Court Fees and Suits Valuation Act, 1958.

12. RELIEF CLAIMED:
    The Plaintiff claims the following reliefs:
    (a) A decree for recovery of Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only) together with interest thereon at the rate of 12% per annum from {{DATE_OF_LOAN}} till the date of realization;
    (b) Costs of the suit;
    (c) Such other relief as this Hon'ble Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

PRAYER:
    It is, therefore, most respectfully prayed that this Hon'ble Court may graciously be pleased to:
    (i) Pass a decree for recovery of Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only) in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant;
    (ii) Award interest at the rate of 12% per annum on the said principal amount from {{DATE_OF_LOAN}} till the date of full realization;
    (iii) Award costs of the suit;
    (iv) Pass any other order or direction as this Hon'ble Court may deem fit and proper in the interest of justice.

VERIFICATION:
    Verified at Bengaluru on this {{VERIFICATION_DATE}} day of {{VERIFICATION_MONTH}}, {{VERIFICATION_YEAR}} that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 8 are true to my personal knowledge and those of paragraphs 9 to 12 are based on legal advice and believed to be true.

    Sd/-
    {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}
    Plaintiff

LIST OF DOCUMENTS:
1. Bank Statement of the Plaintiff evidencing transfer of Rs. 15,00,000/- to the Defendant (Annexure-A)
2. WhatsApp communications between the parties regarding loan and repayment demands (Annexure-B)
3. Legal Notice dated {{DATE_OF_LEGAL_NOTICE}} with proof of service (Annexure-C)

IN THE CITY CIVIL COURT AT BENGALURU
CIVIL SUIT NO. {{SUIT_NUMBER}} OF {{SUIT_YEAR}}

BETWEEN:

{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}},
{{PLAINTIFF_AGE}} years, {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}}
... Plaintiff

AND

{{DEFENDANT_NAME}},
{{DEFENDANT_AGE}} years, {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}}
... Defendant

PLAINT UNDER ORDER VII OF THE CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE, 1908

1. That the Plaintiff is {{PLAINTIFF_DESCRIPTION}}, and is entitled to maintain this suit.

2. That the Defendant is {{DEFENDANT_DESCRIPTION}}.

3. That the Plaintiff and Defendant are known to each other {{RELATIONSHIP_DESCRIPTION}}.

4. That on or about {{DATE_OF_LOAN}}, at {{PLACE_OF_TRANSACTION}}, the Defendant requested the Plaintiff to advance a sum of Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only) as a hand loan for {{PURPOSE_OF_LOAN}}. The Plaintiff expressly states that no written agreement, promissory note, or any other document was executed by the parties in respect of the said loan, and the entire transaction was oral and based on mutual trust and confidence between the parties.

5. That pursuant to the said oral agreement and request, the Plaintiff on {{DATE_OF_LOAN}} transferred the sum of Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only) to the Defendant's bank account bearing number {{DEFENDANT_ACCOUNT_NUMBER}} maintained with {{BANK_NAME_AND_BRANCH}} through {{TRANSFER_MODE}} bearing Transaction/Reference ID {{TRANSACTION_ID}}, as evidenced by the bank statement of the Plaintiff showing the corresponding debit entry.

6. That the Defendant orally undertook to repay the said loan on demand together with interest at the rate of 12% per annum from the date of the loan until realization. No written instrument was executed for the repayment of the said loan or for the interest thereon.

7. That subsequently, the Plaintiff required the said amount for his/her personal/business exigencies and made repeated oral demands upon the Defendant for repayment of the principal amount along with accrued interest, commencing from {{DATE_OF_FIRST_DEMAND}} and continuing thereafter at reasonable intervals, particulars and details of which are corroborated by digital communications including WhatsApp messages exchanged between the parties (copies whereof are annexed hereto as Annexure-B).

8. That despite the aforesaid repeated oral demands, the Defendant failed and neglected to repay the loan amount. Consequently, the Plaintiff, through his/her Advocate, served a legal notice dated {{DATE_OF_LEGAL_NOTICE}} upon the Defendant demanding repayment of the principal amount of Rs. 15,00,000/- along with interest at 12% per annum within the time stipulated therein. A true copy of the said legal notice along with the {{MODE_OF_SERVICE}} is annexed hereto as Annexure-C.

9. That the Defendant received the said legal notice on {{DATE_OF_RECEIPT_OF_NOTICE}} but failed, neglected and/or refused to make any payment towards the principal or interest despite the expiry of the stipulated period, and has thus failed to discharge his/her legal obligation to repay the hand loan.

10. CAUSE OF ACTION:
    The cause of action first arose on {{DATE_OF_LOAN}} when the Defendant received the loan amount. The cause of action further arose on {{DATE_OF_FIRST_DEMAND}} and subsequent dates when oral demands were made and refused, and finally accrued on {{DATE_OF_RECEIPT_OF_NOTICE}} when the Defendant failed to comply with the legal notice demanding repayment. The present suit is filed well within the period of limitation prescribed under Article 21 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, which provides three years from the date when the loan was made.

11. JURISDICTION:
    This Hon'ble Court has territorial jurisdiction as the cause of action wholly or in part arose within the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court at {{PLACE_OF_CAUSE_OF_ACTION}}, Bengaluru. This Hon'ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to entertain and try the present suit as the value of the subject-matter of Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only) together with interest is within the pecuniary limits of the City Civil Court, Bengaluru.

12. VALUATION:
    The value of the subject-matter of the suit for the purpose of jurisdiction is Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only) together with interest thereon as claimed hereinbelow. For the purpose of court-fees, the suit is valued at Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only) and ad valorem court-fee has been paid thereon in accordance with the Karnataka Court Fees and Suits Valuation Act, 1958.

13. RELIEF CLAIMED:
    The Plaintiff claims the following reliefs:
    (a) A decree for recovery of Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only) together with interest thereon at the rate of 12% per annum from {{DATE_OF_LOAN}} till the date of realization;
    (b) Costs of the suit;
    (c) Such other relief as this Hon'ble Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

PRAYER:
    It is, therefore, most respectfully prayed that this Hon'ble Court may graciously be pleased to:
    (i) Pass a decree for recovery of Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only) in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant;
    (ii) Award interest at the rate of 12% per annum on the said principal amount from {{DATE_OF_LOAN}} till the date of full realization;
    (iii) Award costs of the suit to the Plaintiff;
    (iv) Pass any other order or direction as this Hon'ble Court may deem fit and proper in the interest of justice.

VERIFICATION:
    Verified at Bengaluru on this {{VERIFICATION_DATE}} day of {{VERIFICATION_MONTH}}, {{VERIFICATION_YEAR}} that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 9 are true to my personal knowledge and those of paragraphs 10 to 13 are based on legal advice and believed to be true.

    Sd/-
    {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}
    Plaintiff

LIST OF DOCUMENTS:
1. Bank Statement of the Plaintiff evidencing transfer of Rs. 15,00,000/- to the Defendant (Annexure-A)
2. WhatsApp communications between the parties regarding loan and repayment demands (Annexure-B)
3. Legal Notice dated {{DATE_OF_LEGAL_NOTICE}} with proof of service (Annexure-C)

Place: Bengaluru
Date: {{DATE_OF_FILING}}

ADVOCATE FOR PLAINTIFF
{{ADVOCATE_NAME}}
{{ADVOCATE_ADDRESS}}
